programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A look at the fate of United Airlines Flight 232 which crashed into Sioux City's runway in 1989; it caused a subsequent explosion that left 111 people dead.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Seeing how international investigative teams must overcome differences to solve controversial plane crashes.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Investigating the 2009 crash of Colgan Air Flight 3407; the plane stalls at low altitude and dives into a residential area near Buffalo, N.Y.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Moments after takeoff, a plane sustains electrical failures and crashes in the Florida Everglades, killing 110 people on board.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Investigators struggle to determine why a 757 strayed off course during its final approach to a Colombia airport and crashed into a mountain.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When a British tycoon and his family die in a 2017 Australian seaplane crash, investigators spend over two years searching for the reason behind the tragedy.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A plane carrying Mexico's interior minister crashes into Mexico City's financial district.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The 200-year evolution of the submarine in wartime is examined, told by the designers and engineers behind the machines.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: An up-close look at the naval carriers, destroyers, and nuclear submarines of the Cold War.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Stories of historic Black struggle for equality in maritime combat; an enslaved powder boy who served during the French and Indian War; a Civil War gunner; the first African American to command a vessel with a fully integrated crew: Hugh Mulzac.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at how technological advances over the centuries have led to bigger and faster combat ships and transformed maritime warfare.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at key naval battles that have taken place in the Persian Gulf over the last 50 years and the warships involved, from both sides of the conflicts.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at the smallest ships of war and the major roles they've played over the centuries, including the 1776 gunboat built to stop a British invasion and the HA-19 sub.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: New high-tech investigations uncover what sunk a World War I cruiser, a World War II submarine, a British destroyer from the Falklands War, and more.

programme.desc: A look at how navy weapons have evolved over the centuries, from cannons to gun turrets to nuclear missiles and beyond.

programme.desc: Exploring maritime heroes who left their mark on history through their bravery and skill, including Larry Chambers, Ernest Evans, and Robert Smalls.

programme.desc: A look at riverine warfare from the Civil War to Vietnam and small but powerful combat ships.

programme.desc: The history of the F-16 Fighting Falcon and how it became the U.S. Air Force's most versatile plane.

programme.desc: A countdown of the ten greatest military planes of World War II, including the Japanese Zero, the Nazi ME 109, and the B-29 Superfortress.

programme.desc: The inside story of the F-4 Phantom, the U.S. military's go-to fighter for more than four decades.

programme.desc: The world's most versatile and powerful helicopters, including the Huey that served as a rescue helicopter in Vietnam, and the Apache attack helicopter.

programme.desc: An inside look at the U.S. Air Force's largest aircraft, the C-5 Galaxy; its most dramatic combat and peacetime missions over the last 50 years.

programme.desc: The Vietnam-era OV-10 Bronco is pulled out of retirement to join the fight against ISIS.

programme.desc: The history of the Russian MiG, American air power's fiercest rival for more than 50 years.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: Considered the deadliest fighter of WWII, Germany's Me-109s were feared by Allied bombers and fighters alike; 109s fought Spitfires in the Battle of Britain and alongside Rommel in North Africa.

programme.desc: The most-produced bomber in US history, used during World War II by every branch of the American military, as well as by the Royal Air Force.

programme.desc: A look at the amphibious aircraft and the many roles it served during WWII, from patrol bomber to search-and-rescue aircraft.

programme.desc: A look at the British multirole fighter aircraft; the de Havilland Mosquito was used in the Second World War as a bomber, fighter and reconnaissance aircraft.

programme.desc: The overlooked underdog of British fighter planes; fast and versatile, the dogfighter doubled as a bomber and ground support aircraft in every theatre of WWII.

programme.desc: An aerial tour of Nevada; the state's deserts, ghost towns, neon cities and Hoover Dam; its history built on epic booms and busts.

programme.desc: A look at America's landmarks, including a view of bears catching clams in Alaska's rivers; a high-flying tour reveals Alaska's call of the wild spirit that has lured adventurers and dreamers north for centuries.

programme.desc: The world's largest volcanic island; Iceland's landscapes, forged by fire and ice; a tour over Alaska, the residents it attracts and the wildlife that thrives in these unique surroundings.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Three devastating crashes make front-page news around the globe; the media frenzy puts intense pressure on investigators to uncover the causes.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: History's most terrifying air disasters, and the investigations that followed, are revisited and reexamined.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Three flight crews face impossible odds: a Boeing 707 loses engines over the Alps as fire spreads, Air Astana 1388 goes out of control over Portugal, and a bomb on PA 434 severs control cables and blasts a hole in the fuselage.

rating.system: Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía
Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Within five minutes of takeoff, the Dominican Republic bound Birgenair Flight 301 crashes into the Atlantic Ocean; a series of confusing signals crashes the plane.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: The consequences are disastrous when ice forms on an airplane's wings; three planes are brought down by ice, and it's up to investigators to determine how modern aircraft could possibly end up with frozen wings.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A crew argues with no runway in sight; a captain ignores a critical signal; an exhausted pilot picks a catastrophic time to try a new approach.

rating.system: Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía
Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When a 737 careens off the runway in Indonesia, investigators uncover a shocking explanation for the crash.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A look at the fatal consequences that can occur when a fire breaks out on a plane; three flights that erupted in flames and the investigations that changed aviation forever.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: On July 11, 1991, the crew of Nigeria Airways Flight 2120 unsuccessfully tries to return to the airport after a burst tire creates an in-flight fire which causes 247 Nigerian pilgrims and 14 crew to perish.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When the crash of a commuter plane in Georgia kills a NASA astronaut, a four-term U.S. senator and 21 others, the disaster grabs headlines around the world.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: A 1966 landslide in Wales killed 116 children and 28 adults; a 2019 dam collapse killed 115 people in Brazil.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Confused, flying blind and lost in the dark, three crews lose sight of the horizon and fall into the same deadly trap.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Three flights end in tragedy after miscommunication mix-ups and personality clases; Investigators try to unravel what went wrong.
